findings.detail: Clean side-by-side two-column contrast exactly matching intent: 'Human writes the rule' (Person writes rule -> Written-out rule -> New case -> Output) beside 'Machine finds the rule' (Many repeated examples -> Machine spots repeats -> New case -> Output). Columns are height-balanced with parallel ranks, no node overlap, no label truncation, no edge crossings. Subtitles ('same every time' vs 'a likelihood, not a certainty') carry the deterministic-vs-probabilistic contrast straight from the corpus table.

No week-3 ML terms in any label or subtitle: 'model', 'training', 'neural', 'learn' all absent. Machine column stays at corpus-level language ('Machine spots repeats', 'rule lives inside, often unreadable').

stats.notes: 637x606 is the diagram's natural render size; mmdc -w 1400 produces identical dims (intrinsic SVG cap). Subtitles legible at this scale.
